text: The README shows how to set `OPENAI_API_KEY=YOUR_API_KEY_HERE` before running the sample.
label: pass

text: Replace <your-token> with the PAT from your GitHub settings page; never commit the real value.
label: pass

text: Onboarding guide: set STRIPE_SECRET=YOUR_STRIPE_KEY_HERE to your live key, then restart the server.
label: pass

text: Tutorial snippet uses Authorization: Bearer <ACCESS_TOKEN> as a placeholder in the curl example.
label: pass

text: Install step: export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-XXXXXXXX and rerun once the real key is populated.
label: pass

text: config.sample.toml documents token = "REPLACE_ME" so operators know to swap it before deploy.
label: pass

text: curl -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_TOKEN_HERE" https://api.example.com/v1/me in the docs site.
label: pass

text: Helm values example: openaiKey: "<openai-api-key>"  # edit before installing the chart
label: pass

text: Getting-started markdown lists export GOOGLE_API_KEY=YOUR_KEY_HERE as the only env var required.
label: pass

text: Quickstart: set api_key to "your-api-key-here" in settings.json (placeholder stays in the doc only).
label: pass

text: The function get_password loads the value from Vault at runtime; nothing is hardcoded anywhere.
label: pass

text: Security review covered how we store tokens: they live in KMS-encrypted secrets, never in source.
label: pass

text: Our onboarding doc explains that passwords must be at least 14 characters and rotated quarterly.
label: pass

text: The team decided to use an OAuth flow for third-party integrations instead of long-lived keys.
label: pass

text: Password reset emails now include a link rather than a temporary password per the new SSO policy.
label: pass

text: The secret manager is backed by GCP Secret Manager; each token has an audit trail on access.
label: pass

text: Engineering retro noted that credential rotation was slow, so we invested in automation this quarter.
label: pass

text: We agreed the API key documentation page should link to the rotation runbook from the sidebar.
label: pass

text: Our product supports OAuth 2.0 with PKCE for mobile clients, per the integrations handbook.
label: pass

text: SSH key authentication is recommended over passwords for all remote administration paths.
label: pass

text: Encryption at rest uses AES-256-GCM and keys are managed via the customer's KMS integration.
label: pass

text: The CLI supports Bearer authentication and will prompt interactively if no token is cached.
label: pass

text: Passkeys are on the roadmap for next quarter; until then, TOTP + password is the supported flow.
label: pass

text: The JWT session lasts 15 minutes by default and is refreshed transparently by the SDK.
label: pass
